title: Is My Event Location Compliant?
description: Assess whether a proposed event location meets MedTech Europe Code requirements for venue selection.

nodes.text: Is the event taking place in a country where MedTech Europe's Code of Ethical Business Practice applies?
nodes.helpText: The Code applies to interactions (by Member Companies of MedTech Europe) with Healthcare Professionals (HCPs) in Europe. See Scope chapter.

options.label: Yes
No / Unsure

nodes.text: Is the event venue located in a city or town that is a recognised scientific, educational, or business centre?
nodes.helpText: Venues should be chosen for their appropriateness to the event, and not for their leisure or entertainment attributes.

options.label: Yes
No

nodes.text: Does the selected/proposed venue have a reputation primarily as a leisure or entertainment destination (e.g. ski resort, beach resort, theme park)?
nodes.helpText: Even if located near a scientific centre, the venue itself must not be known primarily for leisure.

options.label: Yes — it is primarily a leisure venue
No — it is appropriate for a professional event

nodes.text: Is the event taking place during the peak tourist season for this location?
nodes.helpText: Events in seasonal vacation destinations during peak season may create the appearance of impropriety, even if the venue is otherwise appropriate. In Europe, the ski season is considered to run from December 20 - March 31 and the summer season from June 15 - September 15. Equivalent, seasonally adjusted dates apply in other regions of the world.

options.label: Yes — during peak season
No — outside peak season

nodes.text: The MedTech Europe Code may not directly apply, but local regulations and your company's own code of conduct should still be followed.
nodes.reference: Scope — Geographic applicability of the Code

nodes.text: The venue location does not meet Code requirements. Events must be held in recognised scientific, educational, or business centres.
nodes.reference: Chapter 1, Section 2 — Event Location and Venue

nodes.text: Venues known primarily for leisure or entertainment are not compliant, regardless of their geographic location.
Holding an event during peak tourist season at a vacation-associated location creates an appearance of impropriety.
Based on these criteria, the event location appears to meet Code requirements. Ensure all other event criteria (hospitality, faculty, etc.) are also compliant.

title: Is My Consulting Arrangement Compliant?
description: Evaluate whether a proposed consulting arrangement with a Healthcare Professional meets Code requirements.

nodes.text: Is there a documented, legitimate business need for the consulting services that is identified in advance?
nodes.helpText: The need for the services must be clearly identified before selecting the consultant.

options.label: Yes
No

nodes.text: Was the Healthcare Professional selected based on their qualifications and expertise relevant to the identified need?
nodes.helpText: Selection must be based on qualifications, not on past or potential purchasing decisions.

options.label: Yes — selected on merit
No — selected for other reasons

nodes.text: Is there a written agreement signed by both parties that specifies the nature of the services, the deliverables, and the compensation?
nodes.helpText: A written contract must be in place before services begin.

options.label: Yes
No

nodes.text: Is the compensation reasonable and reflective of fair market value for the services provided?
nodes.helpText: Compensation must not be excessive and should reflect the consultant's actual time and expertise.

options.label: Yes — fair market value
No — may be excessive

nodes.text: A consulting arrangement must be based on a documented, legitimate business need identified before the consultant is selected.
Consultants must be selected based on their qualifications and expertise, not on volume or value of business they generate.
A written agreement specifying services, deliverables, and compensation must be signed before services begin.
Compensation must reflect fair market value. Excessive payments may be viewed as an inducement.
Based on these criteria, the consulting arrangement appears to meet Code requirements. Ensure proper documentation is maintained throughout the engagement.

title: Direct Support to HCP Participation in Events
description: Determine if direct support for Healthcare Professional (HCP) attendance at an event is compliant based on Annex VI rules.

nodes.text: What type of event is the direct support intended for?
nodes.helpText: Support rules differ significantly depending on the event type.

options.label: Third Party Organised Educational Conference (TPOE)
Third Party Organised Procedure Training meeting
Company Event

nodes.text: What is the specific setting within the Third Party Organised Educational Conference?
nodes.helpText: Support restrictions apply differently to the main event versus satellite symposia or booths.

options.label: Main Event / Independent Scientific Program
Satellite Symposium
Booth

nodes.text: What is the intended role of the Healthcare Professional at the Satellite Symposium or Booth?
nodes.helpText: Faculty are those considered speakers or moderators needing preparation. Delegates attend passively.

options.label: Faculty / Speaker
Delegate

nodes.text: What type of Company Event is it?
nodes.helpText: Identify if the primary purpose is procedural training or general product/business promotion.

options.label: Product and Procedure Training and Education Event
Sales, Promotional and Other Business Meeting

nodes.text: Is the Product and Procedure Training and Education Event taking place at or about the same time as a Third Party Organised Educational Event?
nodes.helpText: Proximity to third-party events alters the compliance logic.

options.label: No (NOT at the same time)
Yes (At or about the same time)

nodes.text: What is the intended role of the Healthcare Professional across the events?
nodes.helpText: Faculty/Speakers present or moderate. Delegates attend passively.

options.label: Faculty / Speaker
Delegate

nodes.text: Is the Sales, Promotional and Other Business Meeting taking place at or about the same time as a Third Party Organised Educational Event?
nodes.helpText: Proximity to third-party events alters the compliance logic.

options.label: No (NOT at the same time)
Yes (At or about the same time)

nodes.text: What is the intended role of the Healthcare Professional?
nodes.helpText: Faculty/Speakers present or moderate. Delegates attend passively.

options.label: Faculty / Speaker
Delegate

nodes.text: What is the intended role of the Healthcare Professional across the events?
nodes.helpText: Faculty/Speakers present or moderate. Delegates attend passively.

options.label: Faculty / Speaker
Delegate

nodes.text: Direct support for HCP attendance at the Main Event / Independent Scientific Program of a Third Party Organised Educational Conference is strictly prohibited for both Faculty/Speakers and Delegates.
nodes.reference: Annex VI — Third Party Organised Educational Conference

nodes.text: Direct support for HCP attendance at a Third Party Organised Procedure Training meeting is allowed for both Faculty/Speakers and Delegates.
nodes.reference: Annex VI — Third Party Organised Procedure Training meeting

nodes.text: Direct support for a Product and Procedure Training and Education Event that is NOT taking place at or about the same time as a TPOE is allowed for both Faculty/Speakers and Delegates.
Direct support for Delegates attending a Sales, Promotional and Other Business Meeting is generally NOT ALLOWED. Exception: Allowed ONLY for demonstration of non-portable equipment.
Direct support for HCP attendance is ALLOWED for Faculty/Speakers.
nodes.reference: Annex VI — Direct support for HCP attendance

nodes.text: Direct support for HCP attendance is ALLOWED for Faculty/Speakers, provided a consulting agreement is required and in place.
nodes.reference: Annex VI — Direct support for HCP attendance

nodes.text: Direct support for HCP attendance is NOT ALLOWED for Delegates in this scenario.
nodes.reference: Annex VI — Direct support for HCP attendance

title: When are CVS assessments required?
description: Determine if a Third Party Organised Educational Event requires prior submission to the Conference Vetting System (CVS).

nodes.text: What type of support or activity is the Member Company planning?
nodes.helpText: Select the primary category of support provided to the Third Party Organised Educational Event.

options.label: Educational Grant
Commercial Activity
Direct Sponsorship of HCPs

nodes.text: What is the specific nature of the Educational Grant?

options.label: To support the general running of a conference
To support HCP attendance to the conference
To support Faculty

nodes.text: What is the specific Commercial Activity?

options.label: Consultancy agreement for speakers in satellite symposia
Booths / advertising
Attendance of Member Company Representatives

nodes.text: What is the location and audience scope of the event?
nodes.helpText: Identify if the event is National or International, and if it is inside or outside the MedTech Europe Geographic Area.

options.label: IN MTE Area: National (delegates are local HCPs only)
IN MTE Area: International (delegates from >=2 MTE area countries)
OUTSIDE MTE Area: International (MTE area HCPs attend)
OUTSIDE MTE Area: International (No MTE area HCPs attend)

nodes.text: What is the location and audience scope of the event?

options.label: IN MTE Area: National (delegates are local HCPs only)
IN MTE Area: International (delegates from >=2 MTE area countries)
OUTSIDE MTE Area: International (MTE area HCPs attend)
OUTSIDE MTE Area: International (No MTE area HCPs attend)

nodes.text: What is the location and audience scope of the event?

options.label: IN MTE Area: National (delegates are local HCPs only)
IN MTE Area: International (delegates from >=2 MTE area countries)
OUTSIDE MTE Area: International (MTE area HCPs attend)
OUTSIDE MTE Area: International (No MTE area HCPs attend)

nodes.text: What is the location and audience scope of the event?

options.label: IN MTE Area: National (delegates are local HCPs only)
IN MTE Area: International (delegates from >=2 MTE area countries)
OUTSIDE MTE Area: International (MTE area HCPs attend)
OUTSIDE MTE Area: International (No MTE area HCPs attend)

nodes.text: What is the location and audience scope of the event?

options.label: IN MTE Area: National (delegates are local HCPs only)
IN MTE Area: International (delegates from >=2 MTE area countries)
OUTSIDE MTE Area: International (MTE area HCPs attend)
OUTSIDE MTE Area: International (No MTE area HCPs attend)

nodes.text: What is the location and audience scope of the event?

options.label: IN MTE Area: National (delegates are local HCPs only)
IN MTE Area: International (delegates from >=2 MTE area countries)
OUTSIDE MTE Area: International (MTE area HCPs attend)
OUTSIDE MTE Area: International (No MTE area HCPs attend)

nodes.text: CVS decission not required, but the Chapter 1, General Criteria for Events still applies. CVS and companies apply the same criteria, the only difference is who makes the asessment
This event is SUBJECT TO CVS DECISION. The Member Company must check the Conference Vetting System.
Allowed. Not subject to CVS decision.
Member Company prior internal review against Code principles required.
Not allowed. Direct sponsorship of HCPs registered and practising in the MedTech Europe Geographic Area is prohibited.
Out of scope of the application of the Code.
Not Applicable / Out of Scope
What is the intended role of the sponsored HCP?

options.label: Delegates (passive participation)
Speaker/Faculty in the main programm

nodes.text: CVS decission not required, direct support generally not permitted, but please check the Direct Sponsorship decission tree
CVS decission REQUIRED, direct support generally NOT permitted, but please check the Direct Sponsorship decission tree

title: Grants and Charitable Donations
description: Determine if you can provide a grant or donation to a specific entity, and evaluate what conditions can be lawfully imposed under the General Criteria.

nodes.text: General Criteria: Who is deciding upon, or approving, the provision of these funds?
nodes.helpText: Chapter 4 explicitly mandates an independent review process to mitigate corruption risks.

options.label: The Sales or Commercial function is making/approving the decision
An independent function (e.g., Legal, Compliance, Finance)

nodes.text: General Criteria: Are these funds contingent in any way on past, present, or potential future purchases, leases, prescriptions, or recommendations of the Member Company’s products?

options.label: Yes, there is a commercial expectation or contingency
No, there is no commercial tie

nodes.text: To what type of entity does the Member Company intend to provide the funds or in-kind support?

options.label: Individual Healthcare Professional (HCP)
An HCP designated charity (in lieu of paying the HCP a professional fee)
For-profit organisation (e.g., standalone commercial PCO)
Patient Organisation
Non-profit Hospital or Healthcare Organisation (HCO)
Charitable or philanthropic organisation

nodes.text: What is the intended purpose of the funds provided to the Healthcare Organisation (HCO)?

options.label: Charitable Donation (philanthropic aid or financial support)
Educational Grant (e.g., supporting third-party events, or fellowships)
To cover the HCO normal overhead or routine operational costs

nodes.text: What is the intended purpose of the funds provided to the charitable/philanthropic organisation?

options.label: Charitable Donation (for philanthropic relief/charity)
Educational Grant (for advancement of medical education)
We are participating in their fundraiser (e.g., charity dinner, golf tournament)

nodes.text: Are these Charitable Donations intended to address a demonstrated Financial Hardship of the non-profit hospital/HCO?
nodes.helpText: Hospitals are not philanthropic entities by nature. Donations to them are strictly limited.

options.label: Yes, there is documented hardship (and it benefits patients or specific needs)
No, it is a general Charitable Donation to support the hospital

nodes.text: Are you inviting Healthcare Professionals (HCPs) to attend the fundraiser at the Member Company expense?

options.label: Yes, we intend to invite HCPs or suggest names to the organiser
No, tickets will be used internally by our employees or returned

nodes.text: What conditions or specifications does the Member Company intend to impose on the Charitable Donation?

options.label: We want to control the specific, final use of the funds by the recipient
We will specify a broad, general purpose (e.g., relief for a specific disaster) without final control
No specific controls; the recipient decides how to apply it within their philanthropic mandate

nodes.text: What conditions or controls does the Member Company intend to impose on the Educational Grant?
nodes.helpText: The Code demands extreme separation between providing funds and directing their use.

options.label: We intend to select, or proactively seek the names of, the individual HCP beneficiaries
We want to direct the educational program content or select the Faculty
We will provide parameters/objective criteria (e.g., specialty, country) for the HCO to allocate funds
No control requested. We simply require a written agreement and proof of intended use

nodes.text: Are the objective criteria or the chosen hospital department so narrow that you can effectively identify the individual HCP beneficiaries upfront?
nodes.helpText: e.g., granting funds to a specific sub-department that only employs one or two specific doctors.

options.label: Yes, the final beneficiaries are practically known to us
No, the ultimate HCP beneficiaries are completely unknown to us

nodes.text: NOT ALLOWED. The Member Company sales and/or commercial function shall not decide upon and/or approve decisions to provide Grants or Charitable Donations. An independent review process is mandatory.
NOT ALLOWED. Grants and Donations shall NEVER be contingent in any way on past, present or potential future purchase, lease, or recommendation of products.
NOT ALLOWED. A Member Company shall not provide Grants or Charitable Donations to individual Healthcare Professionals. Funds must go directly to a qualifying entity.
NOT ALLOWED. It is not appropriate to support the favourite charity of a Healthcare Professional in response to a request by that HCP in lieu of a professional fee.
OUT OF SCOPE. For-profit organisations (like commercial PCOs) do not fall within the definition of Educational Grants or Donations. Such arrangements are commercial sponsorships.
NOT APPLICABLE. The Code does not cover Grants or Charitable Donations to Patient Organisations. Please refer to the MedTech Europe Patient Organisation Guidelines.
NOT ALLOWED. A Member Company cannot make a general Charitable Donation to support an HCO. Hospitals have health functions, not philanthropic functions as their main purposes.
NOT ALLOWED. Member Companies must not cover an HCO normal overhead or routine costs of operation.
NOT ALLOWED. Member Companies shall have no control over the specific, final use of funds they provide as Charitable Donations.
COMPLIANT. Specifying a broad, general purpose (e.g., relief of a specific disaster) is ALLOWED, provided the company carefully exercises NO control over the specific, final use.
COMPLIANT. Charitable Donations to philanthropic entities (or HCOs under demonstrated hardship) are ALLOWED without specific final controls.
NOT ALLOWED. A Member Company shall not invite HCPs to attend a fundraiser at its expense, nor suggest names of HCPs to the sponsoring organisation.
COMPLIANT. Participating in fundraisers is ALLOWED provided tickets are used by employees or returned, and no HCPs are subsidized by the Member Company.
NOT ALLOWED. The receiving HCO must be solely responsible for HCP selection. Member Companies must not proactively seek to receive the names of HCP beneficiaries.
NOT ALLOWED. Member Companies shall not have any detailed involvement in determining the educational program content or selecting Faculty.
NOT ALLOWED. Grants are prohibited if the geographical/specialty criteria or chosen hospital department are so narrow that the final beneficiaries are identifiable upfront.
COMPLIANT. Providing objective criteria to assist the HCO is ALLOWED, provided the metrics are relevant to educational needs and do not effectively identify individual HCPs.
COMPLIANT. Providing Educational Grants is ALLOWED, provided a written agreement secures your right to verify intended use.
